original: Theodosian Code of Roman laws is proclaimed in the Western Empire (first law reforms since 295)
Boniface V begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Georg Alt's German translation of Hartmann Schedel's "The Nuremberg Chronicle" is published
Giovanni Cassini discovers Rhea, a satellite of Saturn
Nahum Tate is appointed the third Poet Laureate by English monarchs William and Mary
Russian/Prussian troops occupy Stralsund
France sets plan to tax clergymen
Continental Congress negotiates a war loan of $181,500 from France
Maryland votes to cede a 10 square mile area for District of Columbia
"Visit from St Nicholas" by C Moore published in Troy (NY) Sentinel
Dutch troops in Antwerp surrender
First Chinese theater opens in the US, the Celestial John in San Francisco
First yacht race across the Atlantic won by the schooner Henrietta owned by Gordon Bennett of New York, first to reach Bishop Rock in the Scilly Isles after racing from NY
Turkey's 1st constitution proclaimed, transferring more power to elected representatives
British Field Marshal Lord Roberts departs Southampton for South Africa for the 2nd Boer War
Tentative Turkish & German treaty on construction of Baghdad railway
As American forces defeat Filipino insurgents and impose civil authority, some Filipinos form a Federal Party with a platform recognizing US sovereignty
1st all-steel passengar railroad coach completed, Altoona, Pennsylvania
Albert I becomes the third King of the Belgians
Aswan Low Dam in Nile begins operation again, after being raised for the first time
World War I: Australian and New Zealand troops arrive in Cairo, Egypt
World War I: Battle of Magdhaba - Allied forces defeat Turkish forces in Egypt's Sinai peninsula
Three British warships come close to Holland
Alice H. Parker patents a gas heating furnace
First hospital ship built to transport wounded naval personnel is launched
Government of Ireland Act / Home Rule Act passes, partitioning Ireland
BBC Radio began daily newscasts
Yankees pitcher Carl Mays sold to Reds for $85,000
KEX-AM in Portland OR begins radio transmissions
NBC sets up a permanent coast-to-coast radio network
Police Bureau of Criminal Alien Investigation started in NYC

Lagny-Pomponne rail disaster: 204 people die and 120 injured in deadliest peacetime rail incident in France when express train bound for Strasbourg crashes at 110 km/h (65 mph) into the rear of an older wooden train bound for Nancy, which was stopped on the tracks on a foggy night, 20 km (12 mi) east of Paris
Colombia becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty
First flight of the Vickers Wellington bomber
Discovery of the first modern coelacanth in South Africa
Finnish counter offensive at Summa
South Australia score 7-821 against Queensland
American forces on Wake Island surrender to Japanese
British troops overrun Benghazi, Libya
Japan begins assault on Rangoon, Burma
Allies air attack on Den Helder, Netherlands
Engelbert Humperdinck's "Hansel & Gretel" becomes 1st complete opera to be telecast (WGRB, Schenectady, New York)
Beginning of 'the harsh winter', contributes to the famine that develops in the Netherlands
Belgian Council of State forms
Highest daily ridership in NYC subway history - 8.8 million fares
University of Tennessee refuses to play Duquesne University after they suggested they may use a black player in their basketball game
Last Belgian communities get electricity
National Football League Championship, LA Memorial Coliseum: Los Angeles Rams beat Cleveland Browns, 24-17; first coast-to-coast televised NFL title game
Alain Bombard arrives in Barbados after 65 days at sea proving his theory that a shipwrecked person could survive with almost no provisions, despite having lost 25 kg (65 lbs) in weight
Dodgers 2nd baseman Jim Gilliam wins NL Rookie of Year
René Coty elected President of the Republic and the French Union on the 13th ballot
First human kidney transplant is performed by Dr. Joseph E. Murray at Peter Bent Brigham Hospital in Boston, Massachusetts
Abdallah Ibrahim forms government in Morocco
Jan De Quay's Dutch government falls
King Saud of Saudi Arabia takes power
KICU TV channel 43 in Visalia-Fresno, CA (IND) begins broadcasting
Train accident in Italy, 70 die
AFL Championship, Jeppesen Stadium, Houston: Dallas Texans beat Houston Oilers, 20-17; Tommy Brooker lands winning 25-yard field goal in overtime
Cuba starts returning US prisoners from Bay of Pigs invasion
Fire on Greek ship Laconia, 128 die
The Beach Boys 1st appearance on "Shindig"
India and Ceylon are hit by a cyclone, killing an estimated 4,850 people
Britain's rock TV show "Ready Steady Go" last program
Brussels: NATO Council accept "Flexible Response" strategy
82 members of the US intelligence ship Pueblo are released by North Korea
First US case of space motion sickness
A Protestant man is shot dead at his home in Belfast, North Ireland
French author Régis Debray freed in Bolivia
NYC World Trade Center reaches highest point (411 m)
USSR performs nuclear test
"Immaculate Reception" Steelers turns around a 7-6 defeat with a last second touchdown reception against Raiders to win 13-7
16 plane crash survivors rescued from Uruguayan Air Force Flight 571 after 72 days on the Andean Mountains, after only surviving through cannibalism
6.25 Earthquake destroys central Managua Nicaragua, 10,000 die
B. S. Chandrasekhar takes 8-79 India v England at Delhi
New York Islanders end 15 games winless streak
6 Persian Gulf nations double their oil prices
French Caravelle airliner crashes in Morocco, 106 killed
Chairman of MLB arbitration panel Peter Seitz rules pitchers Andy Messersmith and Dave McNally to be free agents
NY Islanders greatest shutout lose (8-0) vs Chicago Black Hawks
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Geoffrey Boycott becomes leading run-scorer in Test Cricket with 8033
The United States Environmental Protection Agency announces it has identified dangerous levels of dioxin in the soil of Times Beach, Missouri.
Journal "Science" publishes 1st report on nuclear winter
A twin-engine light plane crashes into a shopping mall after failing a landing attempt at nearby airfield in Concord, California; 7 people die, 77 injured, and $3.5 million in damage to the mall

Experimental airplane Voyager, piloted by Dick Rutan & Jeana Yeager, complete 1st nonstop, round-the-world flight without refueling lands at Edwards Air Force Base in California's Mojave Desert, 9 days after taking off from the same location
Buffalo goaltender Tom Barrasso leads the Sabres to a 5-2 win over the Detroit Red Wings to record his 100th NHL victory; makes him the youngest player to reach milestone at just 22 years, 9 months
Lynette "Squeaky" Fromme, serving a life sentence for attempted assassination of PresidentGerald Ford, escapes from Alderson Prison
Slovenians vote to secede from Yugoslavia
NY Daily News publisher Kevin Maxwell resigns
Baseball owners impose salary cap, fiercely opposed by players
Four women are ordained as priests in Jamaica, the first in 330 years of Anglican history
A Channukah candle is officially lighted in Vatican City for the 1st time to celebrate Hanukkah and to reconcile Roman Catholics and Jews
Terry Nichols found guilty of manslaughter in Oklahoma bombing
US Agriculture Department estimates it costs $149,820 to raise a child to 18
A MQ-1 Predator is shot down by an Iraqi MiG-25, making it the first time in history that an aircraft and a modern unmanned drone had engaged in combat
PetroChina Chuandongbei natural gas field explosion, Guoqiao, Kai, Chongqing, China, killing at least 234.
Macquarie Island in the Southern Ocean is hit by an 8.1 magnitude earthquake
Azerbaijan Airlines Flight 217 from Baku, Azerbaijan, to Aktau, Kazakhstan, crashes shortly after takeoff, killing 23 people
Chad declares a state of war against Sudan following a December 18 attack on Adré, which left about 100 people dead
200 civilians are killed by Syrian government warplanes in Helfaya, Syria
The Seleka rebel coalition takes over Bambari, the third largest town in the Central African Republic

United Nations Security Council adopts a landmark resolution demanding a halt to all Israeli settlement in Palestinian territory occupied since 1967. Resolution 2334 was moved by New Zealand, Malaysia, Senegal and Venezuela and passed 14-0 with a US abstention.

Ugandan-English runner and human rights activist Deo Kato (37) completes trek from Cape Town, South Africa to London, England to highlight the story of human migration;  8,230-mile journey took 518 days
The Peace of Atrecht (now Arras) concluded betweenLouis XIof France and Maximilian of Austria, ending the War of the Burgundian Succession
"The Day of the Dagger" Henri, Duke of Guise, leader of the Catholic League is assassinated by the bodyguards ofKing Henry IIIat the Château de Blois
KingJames II, the last Roman Catholic British monarch flees to France from William of Orange
English astronomerJohn Flamsteedobserves Uranus without realizing it's undiscovered
EmperorCharles VInames Archduchess Maria Elisabeth of Austria land guardian of the Austrian Netherlands
American revolutionary officerBenedict Arnoldcourt-martialed for improper conduct and profiteering
US GeneralGeorge Washingtonresigns his military commission as Commander-in-Chief of the Army to Congress
Thomas Jeffersonwarns of slave revolts in the West Indies
PainterFrancisco Goyaswears an oath of loyalty to Joseph Bonaparte as king of Spain, during the Napoleonic occupation of Spain
"Emma" novel byJane Austenis published by John Murray in London
Union General Ben "Beast" Butler is proclaimed a "felon, outlaw & common enemy of mankind" byJefferson Davis
Vincent van Goghcuts off his left ear with a razor, after argument with fellow painterPaul Gauguin, and sends to a female courtesan for safe keeping
Opera "Hansel and Gretel" by Engelbert Humperdinck and his sister Adelheid Wette premieres in Weimar, conducted byRichard Strauss
Ermanno Wolf-Ferrari's opera "I Gioielli Della Madonna" premieres at the Kurfürstenoper in Berlin, Germany, sung in German as "Der Schmuck der Madonna"
Frank Wedekind's play "Oaha, die Satire der Satire" premieres in Munich
First "KeystoneKops" silent slapstick comedy film, entitled "Hoffmeyer's Legacy" is released
US PresidentWoodrow Wilsonsigns Federal Reserve Act into law - creates the central banking Federal Reserve System
Jerome Kernand Schuyler Greene's musical farce "Very Good Eddie" premieres at the Princess Theatre, NYC; runs for 341 performances
GangsterCarlo Gambinoenters the United States as an illegal immigrant on the SS Vincenzo Florio
Visva-Bharati University is inaugurated in Santiniketan, West Bengal, India, founded byRabindranath Tagore
PopePius XIpleas for peace: encyclical Ubi arcano
German silent film classic "The Last Laugh" directed by F. W. Murnau and starringEmil Janningsis released
Bette Davisarrives in Hollywood under contract to Universal Studios
Montreal Canadiens centerHowie Morenzscores in 3-0 win v Detroit Red Wings; his NHL record 249th career goal
John Van Druten's play "Old Acquaintance" premieres on Broadway in NYC
GeneralBernard Montgomerytold he is appointed commandant for D-day
PopePius XIIencyclical Orientals omnes, about Rutheense church
Transistor invented byJohn Bardeen,Walter H. BrattainandWilliam Shockleyis first publicly demonstrated at Bell Laboratories in Murray Hill, New Jersey
"Cinderella" the first full-length ballet byFrederick Ashtonwith music bySergei Prokofievis first presented by Sadler's Wells Ballet at Covent Garden, London
Test cricket debut for all-time great Australian wicketkeeperWally Groutand future captain and coach Bobby Simpson, 1st Test v South Africa in Johannesburg
Fidel Castroannounces Cuba will release 1,113 prisoners from failed 1961 Bay of Pigs Invasion for $62M worth of food & medical supplies
Frank Borman,Jim Lovell, and William Anders become the first people to orbit the Moon aboard Apollo 8
Ballon d'Or: Milan midfielder Gianni Rivera wins award for best European football player ahead of Cagliari forward Luigi Riva and Bayern Munich strikerGerd Müller
7,511th performance ofAgatha Christie's play "The Mousetrap" (record) in London's West End
British Prime MinisterEdward Heathvisits Northern Ireland and expresses his determination to end the violence
US PresidentRichard Nixoncommutes remaining 8 years of Teamsters labor union leader Jimmy Hoffa's 13-year jail term for bribery and fraud
Dmitri Shostakovich' son cycle "Suite on Verses of Michelangelo Buonarroti" premieres in Leningrad
US PresidentGerald Fordsigns Metric Conversion Act in to law
British singer-songwriterCat Stevensformally converts to Islam, taking the name Yusuf Islam

NY Islanders centerBryan Trottiersets NHL record scoring 6 points - 4 goals & 2 assists - in 2nd period of 9-4 win over rival NY Rangers; adds goal & assist for game total 8 points; Islanders undefeated home streak stands at 16 games
Fearing arrest by the FBI, organized crime bossWhitey Bulgerflees Boston, and successfully hides from law enforcement for the next 16 years
"As Good as It Gets" film released written and directed by James L. Brooks, starringJack NicholsonandHelen Hunt
Ballon d'Or: Barcelona/Inter's Brazilian strikerRonaldowins his first of 2 best football player in Europe awards ahead of Real Madrid forward Predrag Mijatović and Juventus midfielderZinedine Zidane
Chicago Bulls coachPhil Jacksonis quickest to reach 500 wins (682 games)
Colorado AvalancheJari Kurriis 8th NHLer to score 600 career goals
Washington Capitals left wingAlex Ovechkinscores two goals to move pastGordie Howefor second place on most NHL career goals list in 4-1 win over visiting Winnipeg Jets
Under future Emperor Nikephoros II Phokas, Byzantine troops storm the city of Aleppo and recover the tattered tunic of John the Baptist during the Byzantine-Arab Wars
Joseph Hansom of London receives patent for Hansom cabs

Indian revolutionary underground in Bengal and Punjab, headed by Rash Behari Bose attempt to assassinate Viceroy of India Lord Hardinge, by throwing homemade bomb into Viceroys's Howdah (elephant carriage) during ceremonial procession in Delhi. Although wounded, the Viceroy survives. Investigations lead to the Delhi conspiracy trial.
Dutch communistMarinus van der Lubbesentenced to death for setting the German Reichstag on fire
Margaret Hamilton's costume catches fire while filming "The Wizard of Oz"
